rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, October 27 2011, Line 4, DEFENDANT CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Once summary adjudication was granted in favor of plaintiffs, defendant should have realized that this motion was no longer tenable and taken it off calendar. A motion for summary judgment can only be successful if the moving party shows that it is entitled to judgment in its favor on all claims between the parties against whom summary judgment is sought. The grant of plaintiffs¿ motion for summary adjudication necessarily precludes defendant from making such a showing. To the extent that the continued pursuit of this motion is a de facto request to reconsider Judge Woolard¿s order stated at the September 30 hearing, Judge Kahn, who is deciding this motion, is without authority to reconsider the September 30 ruling. Moreover, since defendant has sought only summary judgment, not summary adjudication, any argument directed to less than all of the claims must be rejected. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID NEUBAUER VS. UNUM GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, October 27 2011, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant¿s exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. J.P. MORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss This Instant Action And Complaint For Pltf'S Failure To Obtain A Pre-Filing As Required By Ccp 391.7, Subdivision
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, October 27 2011, Line 11, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK'S Motion To Dismiss This Instant Action And Complaint For Plaintiff's Failure To Obtain A Pre-Filing As Required By CCP 391.7, Subdivision.
